name: Mexican Lentil Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring three cups of broth and three cups of water to a boil in a large pot.
2. Add lentils, bay leaves, and garlic. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes.
3. Add the remaining ingredients except corn meal and reserve broth. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer covered for 30 minutes.
4. Stir in corn meal and cook an additional 15 minutes or until vegetables are tender but not overcooked. Remove from heat and allow to cool.
5. Puree the mixture in batches.
6. Heat the reserve stock and stir in puree. Serve in warmed soup bowls and garnish with a dollop of sour cream (Sour King) and sprigs of cilantro.
7. The soup will thicken as it cools because of the corn meal. When reheated, it returns to its original consistency. Additional broth or water can be added to achieve desired thickness.
